title: marsdenia eriocarpa
intro: Marsdenia eriocarpa, also known as the 'Mormordre' or 'Sapo', is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Marsdenia eriocarpa include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Marsdenia eriocarpa are often made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by extracting the roots and stems to produce a tincture or decoction.
